claim: Terminal-based agent interaction is more performant than GUI-based navigation; CLI commands accomplish in one invocation what takes 20-30 GUI clicks, making terminal the optimal interface for agent evaluation.
actionable: Design agent benchmarks and training data around terminal/CLI workflows; benchmark GUI agents separately as a less performant paradigm for automated computer interaction.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: All agent evaluation follows a standard five-step pattern: start container, give instruction, let agent interact, run tests, return reward; this abstraction enables reusable harness infrastructure.
actionable: When building agent evaluation frameworks, standardize on the instruction-container-tests pattern; this enables code reuse across prompt optimization, RL, and CI/CD use cases.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Harbor is a standardized package for running agent evaluations and optimization at scale with pre-integrated agents (CodeX, Claude, Open Hands), dataset registry, and cloud deployment.
actionable: Use Harbor package to avoid rebuilding evaluation harness infrastructure; standardize evaluation format using Harbor task format for any benchmarking, RL, or prompt optimization work.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Task quality is critical for benchmarks; Terminal-Bench 2 spent 300+ hours verifying each of 89 tasks are solvable, reproducible, and valuable; tasks from games/non-work were excluded.
actionable: When creating evaluation datasets, allocate 3+ hours per task for verification; involve multiple human reviewers and check for reward hacking, trivial solutions, and long-term reproducibility.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Scaling evaluation to thousands of parallel containers requires cloud infrastructure; running locally hits core limits; Harbor/Daytona enable thousands of simultaneous sandboxed rollouts.
actionable: For large-scale agent training or optimization, use cloud platforms (Daytona, Modal, ATV) rather than local machines; Harbor integrates these out-of-box.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Open-source agent development via co-authorship incentives: offering paper co-authorship for quality task contributions yielded 250 submissions with 89 making final benchmark (35% acceptance rate).
actionable: When crowdsourcing high-quality evaluation data in academia, offer co-authorship on resulting papers as incentive; this aligns rewards with quality and builds community.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Harbor format is flexible enough to express any agent task via instruction + container + tests abstraction; agents can interact via terminal, GUI, or any other tool installed in container.
actionable: Use Harbor not just for terminal tasks; you can run GUI agents by installing desktop environments in containers; the abstraction is tool-agnostic.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Specialized agents (Terminus 2) for understanding language model capabilities can outperform general agents (Claude Code, Open Hands) on benchmarks designed for agent evaluation.
actionable: For benchmarking language models themselves, use minimal, model-focused agents; general-purpose agents with extra tooling may not be optimal evaluation vehicles.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark

claim: Scientific computing benchmarks (DNA assembly, spectral analysis, astronomy image processing) are future direction for agent evaluation; scientists spend most time at computers coding, not in lab.
actionable: Build agent evaluation tasks for scientific domains where computer work dominates; partner with domain experts to create high-quality scientific computing benchmarks.
source_title: Terminal-Bench 2.0: the most important coding agent benchmark
